hotspots.title: Manuel Adorni’s illicit enrichment scandal
hotspots.blurb: Argentina’s Chief of Staff Manuel Adorni faces intense scrutiny over allegations of illicit enrichment, including cash payments for home renovations and luxury expenses. The scandal has sparked internal government tensions and a significant drop in President Javier Milei’s approval ratings.
hotspots.affect: This scandal commands disproportionate attention due to its moral outrage framing, perceived hypocrisy in austerity policies, and its potential to destabilize Milei’s government.

hotspots.title: Trump’s Iran war escalation and diplomacy
hotspots.blurb: The Trump administration’s military actions in Iran, including Operation Epic Fury and threats to bomb Iran if it does not reopen the Strait of Hormuz, dominate coverage. Diplomatic efforts, including a potential deal with Iran, are also a major focus, with mixed signals from administration officials.
hotspots.affect: This hotspot is charged due to the high-stakes geopolitical tensions, alarmist framing of potential escalation, and polarized reactions from domestic and international actors.

hotspots.title: Javier Milei’s governance and scandals
hotspots.blurb: Argentine President Javier Milei’s governance style, economic policies, and handling of scandals—particularly the Adorni affair—are under intense scrutiny. His international trips, economic reforms, and clashes with domestic opponents are recurring themes.
hotspots.affect: This hotspot is charged due to the high affective investment in Milei’s polarizing leadership, economic shock therapy, and perceived authoritarian tendencies, alongside corruption scandals.

hotspots.title: AfD’s rise in Germany and internal conflicts
hotspots.blurb: Germany’s far-right Alternative für Deutschland (AfD) continues to gain traction in polls, particularly in Saxony-Anhalt, where it leads with 41%. Internal conflicts, legal threats, and controversial proposals dominate coverage, alongside protests and debates over the party’s influence.
hotspots.affect: This hotspot is charged due to the alarmist framing of AfD’s electoral gains, moral panic over far-right normalization, and polarized contention over immigration and democracy.

hotspots.title: Reform UK’s electoral surge and controversies
hotspots.blurb: Reform UK, led by Nigel Farage, is making significant gains in UK local elections, with projections showing it could become the largest party in several councils. Controversies over candidates’ past statements, Farage’s personal finances, and the party’s stance on immigration dominate coverage.
hotspots.affect: This hotspot is charged due to the high affective investment in Farage’s leadership, polarized contention over immigration, and moral outrage over candidate scandals.
